name: Cerro Verde and Cerro Mojón Loop from Tarancón
name_original: Tarancón — circular por sendas y trialeras del Cerro Verde y Cerro Mojón
description: This moderate 28.9-mile mountain biking route near Tarancón explores the technical singletracks and paths of Cerro Verde and Cerro Mojón.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Tarancón offers diverse terrain across the Castilian-La Mancha landscape. The region features shallow soil over limestone bedrock, with open skies and countryside. Trails explore varied topography, including hills like Cerro Verde and Cerro Mojón, providing both climbs and descents. The area's extensive network of routes caters to different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Tarancón The most popular mountain bike trail is Cerro Verde and Cerro Mojón Loop from Tarancón, a 28.9 miles (46.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 13 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the distinct hills of Cerro Verde and Cerro Mojón. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Cerro Verde Trails – Tarancón loop from Estación de Tarancón, a moderate 23.4 miles (37.7 km) path. This route features varied terrain around the Cerro Verde area. Local mountain bikers also love the The Devil's Path – Tarancón Fountain loop from Estación de Tarancón, a 11.6 miles (18.6 km) trail leading through open countryside, often completed in about 1 hour 35 minutes. Mountain biking around Tarancón is defined by limestone bedrock, open countryside, and hills like Cerro Verde and Cerro Mojón. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Tarancón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 180 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Tarancón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Tarancón?
A: There are nearly 50 mountain bike routes around Tarancón, offering a diverse range of experiences. This includes 6 easy routes, 37 moderate routes, and 5 more challenging options.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Tarancón?
A: The terrain around Tarancón is characterized by diverse landscapes, including shallow soil over limestone bedrock, open countryside, and hills like Cerro Verde and Cerro Mojón. You'll find a mix of technical singletracks, wider paths, and routes with significant climbs and descents, suitable for various skill levels.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Tarancón?
A: Yes, Tarancón offers several easy mountain bike trails. An excellent option for beginners or families is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33875444" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Devil's Path – Tarancón Fountain loop from Estación de Tarancón&lt;/a&gt;, an 18.6 km route that takes about 1 hour 35 minutes to complete through open countryside.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see while mountain biking in Tarancón?
A: While mountain biking in Tarancón, you can encounter several interesting landmarks. The historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2953628" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cross of the Order of Santiago&lt;/a&gt; is a significant point. You might also pass by the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5210277" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arco de la Malena and Church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción&lt;/a&gt; in Tarancón itself. The wider region also features the historic Monastery of Uclés and the Archaeological Park of Segóbriga.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Tarancón?
A: The mountain bike trails in Tarancón are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 10 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the open countryside, and the distinct hills like Cerro Verde and Cerro Mojón that offer both climbs and descents.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that offer views of historical sites like the Monastery of Uclés?
A: Yes, there are routes that provide views of historical sites. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20135652" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Monastery of Uclés – Fountain of the Five Spouts loop from Estación de Tarancón&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 42 km route that offers glimpses of the historic Monastery of Uclés.

Q: Are there any loop mountain bike trails in Tarancón?
A: Many of the mountain bike trails in Tarancón are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017431261" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Verde and Cerro Mojón Loop from Tarancón&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38252613" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tarancón – Tarancón Fountain loop from Tarancón&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is a good moderate mountain bike route for exploring the local hills?
A: For a moderate route exploring the local hills, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20135776" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Verde Trails – Tarancón loop from Estación de Tarancón&lt;/a&gt;. This 37.7 km path features varied terrain around the Cerro Verde area and takes approximately 3 hours 30 minutes to complete.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Tarancón?
A: Tarancón is located in a semi-arid climate zone, often referred to as 'España Seca' (dry Spain). While trails are generally clear, spring and autumn offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential chill of winter.

Q: Are there any specific areas known for more challenging mountain biking in Tarancón?
A: The region offers routes with significant elevation changes and varied topography, particularly around hills like Cerro Verde and Cerro Mojón. While specific 'difficult' routes are available, many moderate trails also incorporate challenging sections, providing a good test for experienced riders.

Q: Is it possible to combine mountain biking with other outdoor activities in the Tarancón area?
A: Yes, Tarancón supports a range of outdoor activities. Beyond mountain biking, the area is suitable for e-biking, adaptive biking, hiking, and trail running. The presence of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5412567" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de Uclés&lt;/a&gt;, a pilgrimage route, also suggests opportunities for walking and exploring the cultural landscape.
tips: In addition to being a paradise for lovers of trails and trails, Tarancón offers several places to visit. Among them are the Plaza de la Constitución next to the Town Hall, the Malena arch and just behind the Church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción, the sculptures of Don Quixote inside the Palace of Dukes of Riánsares and within it the caves that are worthy of visit.

Tarancón has a lot to offer to mtb/btt lovers. With a great variety of paths through Cerro Verde, Barranco de los Charcos and Cerro del Mojón, it will be impossible for you to get bored of traveling kilometers and kilometers of very cool and entertaining trails and trial tracks.

Shortly before entering the trails of Cerro Mojón, the path that reaches it is very entertaining and easy to ride with a good surface and some slopes that prepare you for the ups and downs that lie ahead on the hill.

faqs.question: What level of fitness is required for this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate and requires a good physical condition to complete. It spans 46.6 kilometers with approximately 690 meters of elevation gain, taking around 4 hours and 14 minutes. It's not considered beginner-friendly due to its technical sections.

faqs.question: Is this a circular mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the 'Tarancón — circular por sendas y trialeras del Cerro Verde y Cerro Mojón' is a circular route, offering a varied experience as it winds through different landscapes.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, the route starts and ends in Tarancón. You should look for suitable parking within the town or near the initial trailhead to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route features a mix of narrow paths ('sendas') and challenging singletrack sections ('trialeras') that demand skill and control. You can expect potentially slippery terrain, some ravines or gullies, and wooded areas where 'pinos te acarician la cara' (pines caress your face).

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride the Tarancón — circular por sendas y trialeras del Cerro Verde y Cerro Mojón route?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this route would generally be during spring or autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring adverse weather conditions, making some sections more challenging or less enjoyable.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers scenic views from elevated points like Cerro Verde Trails and Trail to Cerro Mojón. You'll also pass through Tarancón itself.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Sendero de la Vía Negrín, Glorieta de la Estación, and Camino de Cueva Romerillo.

faqs.question: Can I bring my dog on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: Information regarding dog-friendliness for this specific route is not available. It's always best to check local regulations or signage at the trailhead regarding pets before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to ride the 'Tarancón — circular por sendas y trialeras del Cerro Verde y Cerro Mojón' route.

faqs.question: What gear should I bring for this mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: Given the route's moderate difficulty and technical sections, it's recommended to bring a well-maintained mountain bike, appropriate safety gear (helmet, gloves), sufficient water and snacks, a repair kit, and navigation tools. Wear comfortable clothing suitable for mountain biking.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is not considered suitable for beginners. Its 'moderate' difficulty rating, technical singletrack sections ('trialeras'), and requirement for good physical fitness mean it's best suited for experienced mountain bikers.
